name: harden-repo-for-coding-agents
description: Assess, harden, scaffold, or diagnose a repo's agent-readiness. Triggers: 'make this repo work with Claude Code', 'score our agent-readiness'. Do NOT use to promote observed agent failures into rules (use rules-from-coding-agent-failures) or to design an AI product's evals (use agent-test).

inspired_by.name: AGENTS.md
inspired_by.author: Agentic AI Foundation (Linux Foundation)
inspired_by.contribution: Vendor-neutral operational contract for coding agents; hierarchical discovery; root-to-leaf merge with closest-file precedence.

inspired_by.name: Agent Skills (agentskills.io)
inspired_by.author: Anthropic
inspired_by.contribution: SKILL.md format with YAML frontmatter; progressive disclosure (~100-token metadata at startup, full instructions on trigger).

inspired_by.name: Model Context Protocol
inspired_by.author: Anthropic / Linux Foundation
inspired_by.contribution: Capabilities-as-API: tools, resources, prompts with typed inputSchema; eliminates context-window inflation from tool definitions.

inspired_by.name: OWASP LLM and Agent Top 10
inspired_by.author: OWASP Foundation
inspired_by.contribution: Threat model: prompt injection, tool abuse, privilege escalation, data exfiltration as primary risks for agents.

inspired_by.name: AI Risk Management Framework (and Generative AI Profile)
inspired_by.author: NIST
inspired_by.contribution: Governance, content provenance, pre-deployment testing, incident disclosure; AI risk differs from traditional software risk in opacity, reproducibility, drift.

inspired_by.name: Don't Build Multi-Agents
inspired_by.author: Walden Yan (Cognition)
inspired_by.contribution: Single-agent linear default; share full context or expect divergent decisions; multi-agent architectures require strong handoff artifacts to survive.

inspired_by.name: Evaluating AGENTS.md: Are Repository-Level Context Files Helpful for Coding Agents?
inspired_by.author: Mündler et al. (ETH Zürich / LogicStar.ai)
inspired_by.contribution: Empirical: autogenerated context files drop task success ~3% and inflate cost >20%; developer-written files improve by only ~4%. Hand-curate, don't /init.

inspired_by.name: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents
inspired_by.author: Anthropic
inspired_by.contribution: Token budget framing: find smallest set of high-signal tokens that maximize likelihood of desired outcome.

inspired_by.name: Harness Engineering: Leveraging Codex in an Agent-First World
inspired_by.author: Ryan Lopopolo (OpenAI)
inspired_by.contribution: Harness is the leverage point, not the model. ~1500 PRs by 3 engineers; trace-based self-verification.

inspired_by.name: GitHub Spec Kit
inspired_by.author: GitHub
inspired_by.contribution: Constitution → Specify → Plan → Tasks → Implement flow; spec-as-source-of-truth.

inspired_by.name: BMAD-METHOD
inspired_by.author: BMAD community
inspired_by.contribution: Multi-persona agent orchestration with explicit handoff artifacts (PM, Architect, Dev, QA, SM, UX, Tech Writer).

inspired_by.name: SWE-agent
inspired_by.author: Princeton NLP
inspired_by.contribution: .traj JSON trajectories; replayable execution histories for debugging, auditing, evaluation.

inspired_by.name: Aider Repo Map (Tree-sitter + PageRank)
inspired_by.author: Paul Gauthier (Aider)
inspired_by.contribution: AST-based dependency graph + PageRank centrality + binary-search token budgeting for repo navigation.

inspired_by.name: Repository Intelligence Graph: Deterministic Architectural Map for LLM Code Assistants
inspired_by.author: Tsvi Cherny-Shahar, Amiram Yehudai
inspired_by.contribution: Shows that giving agents a deterministic build/test/dependency structure map (LLM-friendly JSON) improves mean accuracy and reduces completion time; shifts failures from structural misunderstandings toward reasoning over correct structure.

inspired_by.name: RepoGraph: Enhancing AI Software Engineering with Repository-level Code Graph
inspired_by.author: Siru Ouyang et al.
inspired_by.contribution: Repo-level code graphs used as repository-wide navigation improve repo-scale coding performance (e.g., SWE-bench).

inspired_by.name: CodePlan: Repository-level Coding using LLMs and Planning
inspired_by.author: Ramakrishna Bairi et al.
inspired_by.contribution: Frames repository-level coding as a planning problem; uses incremental dependency analysis and change impact analysis to sequence multi-file edits with validity checks.

inspired_by.name: What You See Is What It Does: A Structural Pattern for Legible Software
inspired_by.author: Eagon Meng, Daniel Jackson
inspired_by.contribution: Argues that LLM coders motivate structural patterns that support incrementality, integrity, and transparency via independent concepts and declarative synchronizations.

inspired_by.name: On the Criteria To Be Used in Decomposing Systems into Modules
inspired_by.author: D. L. Parnas
inspired_by.contribution: Frames modules as responsibility assignments and argues for information-hiding decomposition; motivates partitioning multi-agent work along stable interfaces and likely-to-change decisions.

inspired_by.name: Design Rules: The Power of Modularity (Volume 1)
inspired_by.author: Carliss Y. Baldwin, Kim B. Clark
inspired_by.contribution: Formalizes design rules (stable decisions like interfaces/APIs/standards) that decouple subordinate decisions; supports parallel work by keeping design rules stable and modules independent beneath them.

inspired_by.name: Design Rule Hierarchies and Parallelism in Software Development Tasks
inspired_by.author: Sunny Wong, Yuanfang Cai, Giuseppe Valetto, Georgi Simeonov, Kanwarpreet Sethi
inspired_by.contribution: Shows a design-rule hierarchy can predict which modules are parallelizable (same layer) vs coordination-heavy (cross-layer dependencies); motivates multi-agent work partitioning by dependency layers.

inspired_by.name: Exploring the duality between product and organizational architectures: A test of the “mirroring” hypothesis
inspired_by.author: Alan MacCormack, Carliss Baldwin, John Rusnak
inspired_by.contribution: Finds strong evidence that loosely-coupled organizations tend to produce more modular architectures; supports designing for loosely-coupled multi-agent contributions via explicit contracts and modularity.

inspired_by.name: Splitting the Organization and Integrating the Code: Conway’s Law Revisited
inspired_by.author: James D. Herbsleb, Rebecca E. Grinter
inspired_by.contribution: Case study of geographically distributed development showing integration is often the hardest phase; supports investing in explicit interface contracts and integration/CI surfaces when work is split across agents or sites.

inspired_by.name: Hierarchical Evaluation of Software Design Capabilities of Large Language Models of Code
inspired_by.author: Mootez Saad et al.
inspired_by.contribution: Finds coupling reasoning is brittle under noise in open-ended scenarios; motivates enforcing architectural boundaries with structural checks (lints/tests/CI) rather than relying on agent reasoning.

inspired_by.name: Graph of Skills: Dependency-Aware Structural Retrieval for Massive Agent Skills
inspired_by.author: Dawei Liu et al.
inspired_by.contribution: Shows dependency-aware structural retrieval for large skill libraries improves reward while reducing tokens; motivates structure-aware progressive disclosure at scale.

inspired_by.name: SLSA Framework
inspired_by.author: OpenSSF
inspired_by.contribution: Supply-chain provenance levels for release artifacts; build attestations.

inspired_by.name: GitHub Actions secure use reference
inspired_by.author: GitHub
inspired_by.contribution: Runner-trust guidance: GitHub-hosted runners are ephemeral, while self-hosted runners can be persistently compromised by untrusted workflow code. Grounds fork-PR runner selection, token/secret exposure, and required-check parity checks.

inspired_by.name: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions
inspired_by.author: CNCF / OpenTelemetry
inspired_by.contribution: Standard names for trace/log/metric attributes; code.filepath, code.function, code.lineno; correlation across traces, metrics, logs. The OTel GenAI gen_ai.* conventions are the standards-body LLM/agent layer (still Development status).

inspired_by.name: OpenInference
inspired_by.author: Arize AI
inspired_by.contribution: Agent-aware LLM/agent span semantic conventions over the OTel substrate (prompt, completion, model, token counts, tool calls; explicit span kinds incl. GUARDRAIL and EVALUATOR); openinference-instrumentation-* packages. The span layer trace-to-eval reads — distinct from, not fused with, the OTel GenAI conventions.

inspired_by.name: Engineering Agents — Harness Assessment
inspired_by.author: Engineering Agents
inspired_by.contribution: Level 1–5 maturity rubric (Ad hoc → Instructed → Habitat → Spec Architecture → Sovereign); 'weakest discipline is your ceiling.'

metadata.catalog_summary: Assess, harden, scaffold, and diagnose a repository's agent-readiness for AI coding harnesses (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Copilot, Windsurf, Aider). Harness-agnostic and portable-first; no eval/telemetry prerequisite — it scaffolds from project knowledge (stack, layout, commands, invariants). Start here for first-pass scaffolding.
